title: Can Climate-Friendly Eggs Go Mainstream
subtitle: 气候友好型鸡蛋能走进大众市场吗？

paragraphs.en: On a recent sunny day, 13,000 chickens roam over Larry Brown's 40 windswept acres in Shiner, Texas. Some rest in the shade of a parked car. Others drink water with the cows. This all seems random, but it's by design, part of what the $6.1 billion U.S. egg industry bets will be its next big thing: climate-friendly eggs.
paragraphs.zh: 在最近一个阳光明媚的日子里，一万三千只鸡在拉里·布朗位于得克萨斯州夏纳市那四十英亩狂风劲吹的土地上自由漫步。有的在停着的汽车阴影下休憩，有的则与牛群一同饮水。这一切看似随心所欲，实则精心设计，正是价值61亿美元的美国鸡蛋产业押注的下一个重磅产品——气候友好型鸡蛋的一部分。

words.meaning: adj. 受大风吹的；当风的

paragraphs.en: These eggs, which are making their debut now on shelves for as much as $8 a dozen, are still labeled organic and animal-friendly, but they're also from birds that live on farms using regenerative agriculture — special techniques to cultivate rich soils that can trap greenhouse gases. Such eggs could be marketed as helping to fight climate change.
paragraphs.zh: 这些鸡蛋如今首次上架，售价高达每打八美元，它们依然贴着有机和动物友好的标签，但它们还产自采用再生农业方式的农场——再生农业是一种培育肥沃土壤以捕获温室气体的特殊技术。如此一来，这类鸡蛋便可以打着有助于应对气候变化的旗号进行营销。

paragraphs.en: "I'm excited about our progress," says Brown, who harvests eggs for Denver-based NestFresh Eggs and is adding more cover crops that draw worms and crickets for the chickens to eat. The birds' waste then fertilizes fields. Such improvements "allow our hens to forage for higher-quality natural feed that will be good for the land, the hens, and the eggs that we supply to our customers."
paragraphs.zh: 布朗为总部位于丹佛的NestFresh Eggs公司采收鸡蛋，他正在种植更多覆盖作物来吸引蠕虫和蟋蟀供鸡啄食。他说：“我对我们取得的进展感到兴奋。”鸡的排泄物随后又能为田地施肥。这些改进“使我们的母鸡能够觅食到更高质量的天然饲料，这对土地、对母鸡、对我们供应给顾客的鸡蛋都有好处。”

paragraphs.en: The egg industry's push is the first major test of whether animal products from regenerative farms can become the next premium offering. In barely more than a decade, organic eggs went from being dismissed as a niche product in natural foods stores to being sold at Walmart. More recently there were similar doubts about probiotics and plant-based meats, but both have exploded into major supermarket categories.
paragraphs.zh: 鸡蛋产业的此番发力，是对产自再生农场的动物产品能否成为下一个高端品类所进行的首次重大检验。短短十多年间，有机鸡蛋就从被不屑一顾地视为天然食品商店里的小众产品，发展到在沃尔玛上架销售。最近，人们对益生菌和植物肉也曾抱有类似的疑虑，但这两者如今都已迅猛扩张为超市里的主要品类。

paragraphs.en: If the sustainable-egg rollout is successful, it could open the floodgates for regenerative beef, broccoli, and beyond. Regenerative products could be a hard sell, because the concept is tough to define quickly, says Julie Stanton, associate professor of agricultural economics at Pennsylvania State University Brandywine. Such farming also brings minimal, if any, improvement to the food products.
paragraphs.zh: 如果可持续鸡蛋的推广获得成功，它将为再生牛肉、西兰花等更多品类打开闸门。宾夕法尼亚州立大学布兰迪万校区农业经济学副教授朱莉·斯坦顿指出，再生产品可能很难推销，因为这个概念很难一言以蔽之。此外，这种农业模式给食品本身带来的改善即便有，也微乎其微。

paragraphs.en: The industry is betting that the same consumers paying more for premium attributes such as free-range, non-GMO, and pasture-raised eggs will embrace sustainability. Surveys show that younger generations are more concerned about climate change, and some of the success of plant-based meat can be chalked up to shoppers wanting to signal their desire to protect the environment.
paragraphs.zh: 该行业正押注于这一点：那些愿意为散养、非转基因和牧场饲养等高端属性支付更高价格的消费者，同样会拥抱可持续理念。调查显示，年轻一代对气候变化更为关切，而植物肉的部分成功也可归因于消费者希望借此表达自己保护环境的愿望。

paragraphs.en: Young adults "really care about the planet," says John Brunnquell, president of Egg Innovations. "They are absolutely altering the food chain beyond what I think even if they understand what they're doing."
paragraphs.zh: Egg Innovations公司总裁约翰·布伦奎尔表示，年轻人“真的很在乎这个星球”。“哪怕他们未必完全明白自己在做什么，他们也绝对正在以超出我认知的方式改变着整个食物链。”
